content.Text:
[Tue 2026-04-21 06:14 PDT] Create a new dashboard page `dashboard/regression.html` for AgentLens - an "Agent Regression Tracker" that detects performance regressions across agent deployments using statistical analysis.

IMPORTANT: This is a SINGLE self-contained HTML file with inline CSS and JavaScript. Follow the same pattern as other dashboard pages in `dashboard/` (e.g., `cost-anomaly.html`, `correlator.html`). Look at one of them for the HTML structure, nav sidebar pattern, and styling approach. Use the same `styles.css` link and visual theme (dark theme with CSS variables from styles.css).

## Feature Specification

The dashboard should include:

### 1. Deployment Timeline
- Visual timeline showing deployments (versions) with performance metrics
- Color-coded: green (improvement), yellow (neutral), red (regression)
- Click a deployment to see detailed metrics

### 2. Regression Detection Engine (JavaScript)
- Compare consecutive deployment windows using Welch's t-test approximation
- Metrics tracked: latency (p50, p95), error rate, token usage, cost per request, success rate
- Configurable significance level (α = 0.01, 0.05, 0.10)
- Effect size calculation (Cohen's d) to distinguish meaningful vs statistical regressions
- Auto-generates simulated deployment data for demo purposes

### 3. Regression Report Panel
- List of detected regressions sorted by severity (critical/warning/info)
- Each finding shows: metric name, old mean → new mean, % change, p-value, effect size, severity badge
- Proactive recommendations for each regression (e.g., "Latency p95 increased 23% - consider rolling back or investigating model changes")

### 4. Metric Comparison Charts
- Canvas-based before/after distribution overlays for selected metrics
- Sparkline trend across last N deployments for each metric
- Highlight the exact deployment where regression started

### 5. Auto-Monitor Mode
- Toggle button for "continuous monitoring" simulation
- When enabled, new "deployments" appear every few seconds
- Auto-detects and surfaces regressions with notification badges
- Proactive alerts appear as toast notifications

### 6. Proactive Insights
- "Root cause hypotheses" section that correlates regressions across metrics
- E.g., "Latency and token usage both regressed in v2.4.1 - likely a prompt change"
- Confidence scoring for each hypothesis
- Suggested investigation steps

## Navigation
Add a link to regression.html in the sidebar nav. Look at how other pages are linked in the existing HTML files and follow the same pattern.

## Technical Notes
- Pure HTML/CSS/JS, no external dependencies except styles.css
- Generate realistic simulated data (15-20 deployments with version numbers like v2.1.0, v2.2.0, etc.)
- Use Canvas API for charts
- Responsive layout
- All interactive, all client-side

## Build Verification
After creating the file, verify:
1. The HTML is well-formed (check for unclosed tags)
2. No syntax errors in the JavaScript (the file should be parseable)

Do NOT modify any existing files except to add a nav link if you can identify the common nav pattern. If the nav is in app.js or styles.css and complex, skip modifying it - just create the new HTML file.
